metadata.normalization_policy: Sequential idea_id values are assigned by the script after generation to protect downstream M3 mapping.

ideas.title: Modular Combat Architect
ideas.target_user: Core mobile gamers looking for deep strategic customization.
ideas.problem_or_desire: Frustration with limited customization options in existing mobile action games and a desire to create unique combat playstyles.
ideas.core_concept: A real-time action combat game where players can swap and combine hundreds of micro-modules (e.g., weapon attachments, cybernetic augments, projectile modifiers) during a match to dynamically adapt their build against opponents or environmental challenges.
ideas.proposed_approach: Focus on developing an intuitive modular UI for pre-match building and an event-driven system for on-the-fly module integration during gameplay. Utilize procedural generation for visual module variations to expand aesthetic diversity.
ideas.prototype_scope: A single-player PvE challenge mode where players must defeat waves of enemies by progressively swapping and testing five distinct purchasable module types on a base attack, demonstrating the dynamic customization and combat synergy.
ideas.one_sentence_pitch: A real-time action combat game where players can swap and combine hundreds of micro-modules (e.g., weapon attachments, cybernetic augments, projectile modifiers) during a match to dynamically adapt their build against opponents or environmental challenges.
ideas.hidden_pain_or_desire: Frustration with limited customization options in existing mobile action games and a desire to create unique combat playstyles.
ideas.creative_leap: A real-time action combat game where players can swap and combine hundreds of micro-modules (e.g., weapon attachments, cybernetic augments, projectile modifiers) during a match to dynamically adapt their build against opponents or environmental challenges.
ideas.proposed_solution: Focus on developing an intuitive modular UI for pre-match building and an event-driven system for on-the-fly module integration during gameplay. Utilize procedural generation for visual module variations to expand aesthetic diversity.
ideas.core_experience: A real-time action combat game where players can swap and combine hundreds of micro-modules (e.g., weapon attachments, cybernetic augments, projectile modifiers) during a match to dynamically adapt their build against opponents or environmental challenges.
ideas.mvp_seed: A single-player PvE challenge mode where players must defeat waves of enemies by progressively swapping and testing five distinct purchasable module types on a base attack, demonstrating the dynamic customization and combat synergy.
ideas.first_7_day_prototype: A single-player PvE challenge mode where players must defeat waves of enemies by progressively swapping and testing five distinct purchasable module types on a base attack, demonstrating the dynamic customization and combat synergy.

ideas.title: Gravity Sling: Adaptive Platformer
ideas.target_user: Casual mobile players who enjoy puzzle elements within fast-paced action.
ideas.problem_or_desire: A lack of mobile action games that fluidly integrate physics puzzles into core combat and exploration loops, leading to predictable gameplay.
ideas.core_concept: A 2D side-scrolling action game where the player character can alter gravity's direction and intensity within localized zones. This core mechanic is used for navigation, avoiding traps, and manipulating projectiles to defeat enemies.
ideas.proposed_approach: Develop physics engine supporting real-time gravity field manipulation. Implement character movement that interacts intuitively with these changing forces. Design level layouts that explicitly leverage gravity shifts for both offense and defense.
ideas.prototype_scope: A tutorial level and two short adventure stages demonstrating: 1) Using gravity shifts to reach platforms and clear debris, and 2) Using gravity bursts to launch enemies into traps or environmental hazards. No deep enemy AI required.
ideas.one_sentence_pitch: A 2D side-scrolling action game where the player character can alter gravity's direction and intensity within localized zones. This core mechanic is used for navigation, avoiding traps, and manipulating projectiles to defeat enemies.
ideas.hidden_pain_or_desire: A lack of mobile action games that fluidly integrate physics puzzles into core combat and exploration loops, leading to predictable gameplay.

ideas.proposed_solution: Develop physics engine supporting real-time gravity field manipulation. Implement character movement that interacts intuitively with these changing forces. Design level layouts that explicitly leverage gravity shifts for both offense and defense.
ideas.core_experience: A 2D side-scrolling action game where the player character can alter gravity's direction and intensity within localized zones. This core mechanic is used for navigation, avoiding traps, and manipulating projectiles to defeat enemies.
ideas.mvp_seed: A tutorial level and two short adventure stages demonstrating: 1) Using gravity shifts to reach platforms and clear debris, and 2) Using gravity bursts to launch enemies into traps or environmental hazards. No deep enemy AI required.

ideas.title: Kinetic Echo Recall
ideas.target_user: Experienced competitive mobile gamers seeking novel tactical depth.
ideas.problem_or_desire: The meta in competitive mobile action games often becomes stale, and players desire fresh strategic options that reward foresight and timing.
ideas.core_concept: A player-versus-player arena brawler where players can, after a successful execution of a 'Kinetic Echo' (recording a sequence of 3-5 combat actions), replay that entire sequence by releasing a temporal clone. These echoes can flank, distract, or even perform complex attacks.
ideas.proposed_approach: Design AI for echo replication that precisely mirrors player input at specific timings. Balance the cooldown and resource costs for echo deployment and recovery. Develop intuitive player feedback for clone intent and potential threats.
ideas.prototype_scope: Two hero characters in a symmetrical arena. One vs. One mode demonstrating basic combat and the core 'Kinetic Echo' mechanic: perform an echo attack, dodge enemy counter-attack, then trigger the echo sequence to flank the opponent. Limited to 3 sample echo ability types.
ideas.one_sentence_pitch: A player-versus-player arena brawler where players can, after a successful execution of a 'Kinetic Echo' (recording a sequence of 3-5 combat actions), replay that entire sequence by releasing a temporal clone. These echoes can flank, distract, or even perform complex attacks.
ideas.hidden_pain_or_desire: The meta in competitive mobile action games often becomes stale, and players desire fresh strategic options that reward foresight and timing.

ideas.mvp_seed: Two hero characters in a symmetrical arena. One vs. One mode demonstrating basic combat and the core 'Kinetic Echo' mechanic: perform an echo attack, dodge enemy counter-attack, then trigger the echo sequence to flank the opponent. Limited to 3 sample echo ability types.
ideas.first_7_day_prototype: Two hero characters in a symmetrical arena. One vs. One mode demonstrating basic combat and the core 'Kinetic Echo' mechanic: perform an echo attack, dodge enemy counter-attack, then trigger the echo sequence to flank the opponent. Limited to 3 sample echo ability types.

ideas.title: Ephemeral Artificer: The Sand Kingdom
ideas.target_user: Mid-core mobile gamers who appreciate procedural generation and emergent storytelling.
ideas.problem_or_desire: A longing for single-player action adventure games where the game world feels unique and responsive to player actions beyond static encounters, facilitating a sense of authorship.
ideas.core_concept: A 3D open-world action game set in a constantly evolving desert environment. The core mechanic involves 'Erosive Construction,' where player actions (attacks, movements) dynamically sculpt the terrain, creating temporary cover, trapdoors, or new pathways that 'wind away' over time like sand dunes.
ideas.proposed_approach: Employ advanced terrain deformation algorithms that run in near real-time. Implement agent AI that can dynamically pathfind and react to the constantly changing landscape. Design elemental spells that interact procedurally with the shifting sands (e.g., temporary petrification, mini-dust storms).
ideas.prototype_scope: One controllable character exploring a small desert biome segment. Task: reach a specific landmark using terrain manipulation to overcome 3 predefined physical obstacles. Enemies present (a few types), their AI only minimally aware of environmental shifts.
ideas.one_sentence_pitch: A 3D open-world action game set in a constantly evolving desert environment. The core mechanic involves 'Erosive Construction,' where player actions (attacks, movements) dynamically sculpt the terrain, creating temporary cover, trapdoors, or new pathways that 'wind away' over time like sand dunes.

ideas.title: Rhythmic Retaliator: Symphony of Carnage
ideas.target_user: Casual rhythm game players seeking a visceral action experience.
ideas.problem_or_desire: Boredom with standard 'tap-to-attack' or 'virtual joystick' controls; a desire for mobile combat that rewards precision timing and a sense of flow.
ideas.core_concept: A swipe-and-tap combat game where every player action (attacks, dodges, special abilities) must be executed in time with dynamic background music. Consecutive timed inputs increase a combo multiplier and trigger enhanced, spectacular animations and attacks tied to musical beats.
ideas.proposed_approach: Integrate a beat-tracking system linked directly to core attack/defend inputs. Design music tracks with clear rhythmic patterns and tie them procedurally to combat events. Develop responsive animation feedback systems to signal successful timing and combos.
ideas.prototype_scope: A 'score attack' survival mode against 3 enemy archetypes. Three songs of increasing complexity. The prototype focuses solely on demonstrating rhythmic input registration for melee attacks and basic dodges. Success on a song scores points based on timing accuracy and chain combos. No complex ability systems.
ideas.one_sentence_pitch: A swipe-and-tap combat game where every player action (attacks, dodges, special abilities) must be executed in time with dynamic background music. Consecutive timed inputs increase a combo multiplier and trigger enhanced, spectacular animations and attacks tied to musical beats.
